title: Coryphantha radians
intro: Coryphantha radians, also known as the "radial beehive cactus," is a species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its distinctive beehive-shaped stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Coryphantha radians include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Coryphantha radians is often used to prepare topical ointments, salves, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into capsules and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and naturopathic practitioners.
taxonomy: Coryphantha radians belongs to the family Cactaceae, commonly known as the cactus family. This species is a type of pincushion cactus, characterized by its short stems and globular shape. Coryphantha radians is native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It is classified within the genus Coryphantha, which contains over 50 species of pincushion cacti. The classification of Coryphantha radians is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its radial arrangement of areoles and tubercles.
